name: Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular
name_original: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla - circular desde el Castillo de Constantina
description: This difficult 41.4-mile mountain biking route combines the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte with the unique "stone forest" of Cerro del Hierro.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Constantina, nestled within the Sierra Norte Natural Park in Seville, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features varied terrain, including the unique "stone forest" of Cerro del Hierro, the picturesque Huéznar River area, and the accessible Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte. These natural features provide a range of mountain bike trails Constantina, catering to different skill levels and offering significant elevation gains. Best mountain bike trails around Constantina The most popular mountain bike trail is Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular, a difficult 41.4 miles (66.7 km) trail that takes 5 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route combines the Vía Verde with the distinctive natural feature of Cerro del Hierro. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Iron Hill – The mine loop from Cazalla - Constantina, a moderate 26.8 miles (43.1 km) path. This trail explores the mining history and terrain around Iron Hill. Local mountain bikers also love the Small tunnel – Flowing Hueznar River loop from Cazalla - Constantina, a 43.1 miles (69.4 km) trail leading through the Huéznar River landscape, often completed in about 6 hours 24 minutes. Mountain biking around Constantina is defined by the unique Cerro del Hierro, the scenic Huéznar River, and the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Constantina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Constantina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails are available around Constantina?
A: Constantina, located within the Sierra Norte Natural Park, offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails. You'll find routes catering to various skill levels, from easier paths suitable for all fitness levels to more challenging options requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. The terrain often includes unique natural features like the 'stone forest' of Cerro del Hierro, the picturesque Huéznar River area, and sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Constantina?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there are trails suitable for beginners. Out of the 16 mountain bike trails in the area, at least one is classified as easy. These easier routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them a good starting point for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced mountain bikers looking for a challenge will find several difficult routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987268729/via-verde-and-cerro-del-hierro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 41.4-mile (66.7 km) trail that combines the accessible Vía Verde with the distinctive Cerro del Hierro. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small tunnel – Flowing Hueznar River loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 43.1 miles (69.4 km) and explores the scenic Huéznar River landscape.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking near Constantina?
A: The trails around Constantina offer access to several unique natural features and landmarks. You can explore the striking 'stone forest' of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, ride alongside the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/736496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar River Landscape&lt;/a&gt;, and even discover the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Norte de Sevilla Greenway&lt;/a&gt; also provides an accessible route through the park.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that are circular loops?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Constantina are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987268729/via-verde-and-cerro-del-hierro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular&lt;/a&gt;, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron Hill – The mine loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small tunnel – Flowing Hueznar River loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Constantina?
A: The mountain bike trails around Constantina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the unique natural features like Cerro del Hierro, and the scenic beauty of the Huéznar River area. The variety of routes catering to different skill levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can I find trails that explore the unique Cerro del Hierro?
A: Absolutely. Cerro del Hierro, known for its distinctive 'stone forest' landscape, is a prominent feature in several mountain biking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987268729/via-verde-and-cerro-del-hierro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which integrates this natural monument into a longer, challenging ride. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any river beaches or tunnels to explore on mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region offers unique points of interest like river beaches and tunnels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Nicolás del Puerto Beach&lt;/a&gt;, where bathing is permitted, can be a refreshing stop. Additionally, some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small tunnel – Flowing Hueznar River loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt;, lead through areas with tunnels to explore near the Huéznar River, adding an adventurous element to your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Constantina?
A: Constantina offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. While the Castañares Trail is particularly spectacular in autumn when the chestnut leaves change color, spring and early summer also provide pleasant temperatures. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to ride year-round, though checking local weather forecasts is always advisable.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in the Constantina area?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails available around Constantina, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels. These include 1 easy, 9 moderate, and 6 difficult routes, ensuring options for nearly every rider.

Q: Can I find trails that follow the Huéznar River?
A: Yes, the picturesque Huéznar River area is a highlight of the Sierra Norte Natural Park and is featured in several mountain bike loops. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Small tunnel – Flowing Hueznar River loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6887471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Hueznar – Molino del Corcho Route loop from Constantina&lt;/a&gt; allow you to ride alongside this beautiful river, offering scenic views and a rich natural setting.

Q: Are there any trails that incorporate the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte?
A: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte, a 'Greenway,' provides an accessible and enjoyable cycling experience that mountain bikers can integrate into their rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987268729/via-verde-and-cerro-del-hierro-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, combining sections of this greenway with other natural attractions for a longer, more varied route.
tips: The Moorish-era castle was declared a site of cultural interest in 1985. It can be easily reached on foot and by car. The panoramic views of the town are impressive.

A magical stretch of road.
I recommend you cross this forest in the fall: wonderful.

If you pass through here, this is the perfect place to stop and recharge your batteries. The recreational area offers tables, shade and a pleasant natural environment, ideal for relaxing and enjoying a revitalizing break.
The Cerro del Hierro is a hill formed by limestone rich in iron. It was declared a natural monument for its geological and landscape value. It is a popular place for hiking, climbing and cycling.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route, covering approximately 66.6 km, generally takes around 5 hours and 20 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make to explore points of interest, and the specific conditions on the day.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. A significant portion follows the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte, which is built on an old railway line, offering generally flat or gently inclined paths with compacted earth and asphalt surfaces. As you approach the Cerro del Hierro Natural Monument, the scenery shifts dramatically to a 'stone forest' with unique karstic limestone formations. While the Vía Verde section is smooth, exploring the immediate vicinity of Cerro del Hierro might involve slightly more technical, shorter paths through these formations.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the overall tour is rated as 'difficult' due to its length and elevation gain, the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte section is considered easy to low-medium difficulty, with minimal gradients. However, the full circular route, especially incorporating the more technical sections around Cerro del Hierro, requires a good level of fitness and some mountain biking experience. Beginners might prefer to tackle shorter sections of the Vía Verde itself.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of natural beauty and historical significance. You'll pass by the impressive Castle of Constantina, cycle through the scenic Molino del Corcho Route, and can stop at the Molino del Corcho Recreation Area. The highlight is undoubtedly the Cerro del Hierro Natural Monument, with its unique 'stone forest' formations. There are also opportunities for stunning vistas, such as the View of Sierra Morena de Sevilla Natural Park.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this route is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable cycling conditions. Summers can be very hot in Andalucía, and while winters are usually mild, some sections might become muddy after heavy rains.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte section is generally dog-friendly, allowing well-behaved dogs on a leash. When exploring the Cerro del Hierro Natural Monument area, it's also usually permitted to have dogs on a leash. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect any specific signage or local regulations you encounter within the natural park.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route or the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte or to access the Cerro del Hierro Natural Monument. The route is located within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla, which is a protected area. While access is free, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment, stay on marked paths, and adhere to general park regulations regarding waste, noise, and wildlife protection.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular route?
faqs.answer: For this circular route starting near Constantina, you can typically find parking in or around the town of Constantina, which is a common starting point. Specific parking areas for the Vía Verde are often available at former railway stations along the route, such as Cazalla de la Sierra or Constantina. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking spots.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates significant sections of other official trails. You'll spend a considerable portion on the Vía Verde de la Sierra Norte. It also overlaps with parts of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, Alanís Comarcal Norte, and Alanís - Cerro del Hierro - San Nicholás del Puerto. Smaller segments also coincide with the Transandalus and various Sendero Cerro del Hierro paths, allowing for diverse exploration.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restrooms along the Vía Verde and Cerro del Hierro Circular?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find some facilities along the route. The Molino del Corcho Recreation Area is a good spot for a break. Towns like Constantina, where the route begins, offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and shops. Along the Vía Verde, some former station buildings have been converted into visitor centers or small eateries. However, it's always wise to carry enough water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns.

waypoints.name: View of Sierra Morena de Sevilla Natural Park
